questions.text: What is the primary purpose of Private cloud in virtualization and cloud computing?

answers.text: A private cloud is a cloud environment dedicated to one organization, giving that organization greater control over access, security, configuration, and resource placement.
In this scenario, a public cloud is a provider-owned cloud environment that delivers shared computing services to multiple customers over the internet.
In this scenario, a hybrid cloud combines private cloud, public cloud, or on-premises resources so workloads and data can move or integrate across environments.
In this scenario, a community cloud is shared by organizations with common requirements, such as regulatory, security, mission, or industry needs.

explanations.a: A private cloud is a cloud environment dedicated to one organization, giving that organization greater control over access, security, configuration, and resource placement. Private cloud is chosen when control and dedicated use matter more than broad public-provider sharing. This is the correct answer.
explanations.b: Public cloud means a public cloud is a provider-owned cloud environment that delivers shared computing services to multiple customers over the internet. Private cloud means a private cloud is a cloud environment dedicated to one organization, giving that organization greater control over access, security, configuration, and resource placement.
explanations.c: Hybrid cloud means a hybrid cloud combines private cloud, public cloud, or on-premises resources so workloads and data can move or integrate across environments. Private cloud means a private cloud is a cloud environment dedicated to one organization, giving that organization greater control over access, security, configuration, and resource placement.
explanations.d: Community cloud means a community cloud is shared by organizations with common requirements, such as regulatory, security, mission, or industry needs. Private cloud means a private cloud is a cloud environment dedicated to one organization, giving that organization greater control over access, security, configuration, and resource placement.
